lede: A look at May 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Phil Foden and Kostas Mendrinos, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Formula One
events.desc: Former Formula One driver Takuma Sato wins his first Indianapolis 500, the first Japanese and Asian driver to do so. Double world champion Fernando Alonso retires from the race with an engine issue in his first entry of the event.

events.title: Harambe
events.desc: Harambe, a gorilla, is shot to death after grabbing a three-year-old boy in his enclosure at the Cincinnati Zoo and Botanical Garden, resulting in widespread criticism and sparking various internet memes.

events.title: Milan
events.desc: In Milan, Italy, after 22 years of restoration work, Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece The Last Supper is put back on display.

events.title: Mathias Rust
events.desc: An 18-year-old West German pilot, Mathias Rust, evades Soviet Union air defences and lands a private plane in Red Square in Moscow, Russia.

events.title: Northern Ireland
events.desc: Northern Ireland's power-sharing Sunningdale Agreement collapses following a general strike by loyalists.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Belgium surrenders to Nazi Germany to end the Battle of Belgium.

events.title: Alan Turing
events.desc: Alan Turing submits On Computable Numbers for publication.

events.title: President of the United States
events.desc: U.S. President Andrew Jackson signs the Indian Removal Act which denies Native Americans their land rights and forcibly relocates them.

events.title: French and Indian War
events.desc: French and Indian War: In the first engagement of the war, Virginia militia under the 22-year-old Lieutenant colonel George Washington defeat a French reconnaissance party in the Battle of Jumonville Glen in what is now Fayette County in southwestern Pennsylvania.

events.title: English Civil War
events.desc: English Civil War: Bolton Massacre by Royalist troops under the command of James Stanley, 7th Earl of Derby.

deaths.text: Claude Lemieux, heavy-hitting retired NHL forward and four-time Stanley Cup winner
George Irving Bell, American physicist, biologist, and mountaineer
Audie Murphy, American soldier and actor, Medal of Honor recipient
